title: abutilon mauritianum subsp. zanzibaricum
intro: Abutilon mauritianum subsp. zanzibaricum, commonly known as the Zanzibar thornapple, is a plant species native to East Africa, belonging to the Malvaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The leaves and roots of the plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal medicine, preparations of Abutilon mauritianum subsp. zanzibaricum are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
